channel.title: Glyph & Cog, LLC — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Glyph & Cog, LLC (gandc).

item.title: CVE-2026-4407 · 2.1 LOW — Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.06 due to missing validation
item.description: Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.06 and earlier, due to incorrect validation of the "N" field in ICCBased color spaces.

item.title: CVE-2025-11896 · 2.1 LOW — Stack overflow in Xpdf 4.05 due to object loop in PDF CMap
item.description: In Xpdf 4.05 (and earlier), a PDF object loop in a CMap, via the "UseCMap" entry, leads to infinite recursion and a stack overflow.

item.title: CVE-2025-3154 · 2.1 LOW — Out-of-bounds array write due to invalid VerticesPerRow in Xpdf 4.05
item.description: Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.05 and earlier, triggered by an invalid VerticesPerRow value in a PDF shading dictionary.

item.title: CVE-2025-2574 · 2.1 LOW — Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.05 due to incorrect integer overflow checking
item.description: Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.05 and earlier, due to incorrect integer overflow checking in the PostScript function interpreter code.

item.title: CVE-2024-7868 · 2.1 LOW — Uninitialized variable in Xpdf 4.05 due to invalid JPEG header
item.description: In Xpdf 4.05 (and earlier), invalid header info in a DCT (JPEG) stream can lead to an uninitialized variable in the DCT decoder. The proof-of-concept PDF file causes a segfault attempting to read from an invalid address.

item.title: CVE-2024-7867 · 2.1 LOW — Integer overflow and divide-by-zero in Xpdf 4.05 due to bogus page box coordinates
item.description: In Xpdf 4.05 (and earlier), very large coordinates in a page box can cause an integer overflow and divide-by-zero.

item.title: CVE-2024-7866 · 2.1 LOW — Stack overflow in Xpdf 4.05 due to object loop in PDF pattern
item.description: In Xpdf 4.05 (and earlier), a PDF object loop in a pattern resource leads to infinite recursion and a stack overflow.

item.title: CVE-2024-4976 · 2.1 LOW — Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.05 due to missing object type check
item.description: Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.05 and earlier, due to missing object type check in AcroForm field reference.

item.title: CVE-2024-4568 · 2.9 LOW — Stack overflow in Xpdf 4.05 due to object loop in PDF resources
item.description: In Xpdf 4.05 (and earlier), a PDF object loop in the PDF resources leads to infinite recursion and a stack overflow.

item.title: CVE-2024-4141 · 2.9 LOW — Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.05 due to incorrect bounds check
item.description: Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.05 and earlier, triggered by an invalid character code in a Type 1 font. The root problem was a bounds check that was being optimized away by modern compilers.

item.title: CVE-2024-3900 · 2.9 LOW — Out-of-bounds stack array write in Xpdf 4.05 due to missing zero check
item.description: Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.05 and earlier, triggered by long Unicode sequence in ActualText.

item.title: CVE-2024-3248 · 2.9 LOW — Stack overflow in Xpdf 4.05 due to object loop in attachments
item.description: In Xpdf 4.05 (and earlier), a PDF object loop in the attachments leads to infinite recursion and a stack overflow.

item.title: CVE-2024-3247 · 2.9 LOW — Stack overflow in Xpdf 4.05 due to object loop in PDF object stream
item.description: In Xpdf 4.05 (and earlier), a PDF object loop in an object stream leads to infinite recursion and a stack overflow.

item.title: CVE-2024-2971 · 2.9 LOW — Out-of-bounds array access due to negative object numbers in indirect references in Xpdf 4.05
item.description: Out-of-bounds array write in Xpdf 4.05 and earlier, triggered by negative object number in indirect reference in the input PDF file.

item.title: CVE-2023-3436 · 3.3 LOW — Deadlock in Xpdf 4.04 due to PDF object stream references
item.description: Xpdf 4.04 will deadlock on a PDF object stream whose "Length" field is itself in another object stream.

item.title: CVE-2023-3044 · 3.3 LOW — Divide-by-zero in Xpdf 4.04 due to very large page size
item.description: An excessively large PDF page size (found in fuzz testing, unlikely in normal PDF files) can result in a divide-by-zero in Xpdf's text extraction code. This is related to CVE-2022-30524, but the problem here is caused by a very large page size, rathe…

item.title: CVE-2023-2664 · 2.9 LOW — Stack overflow in Xpdf 4.04 due to object loop in PDF embedded file tree
item.description: In Xpdf 4.04 (and earlier), a PDF object loop in the embedded file tree leads to infinite recursion and a stack overflow.

item.title: CVE-2023-2663 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Stack overflow in Xpdf 4.04 due to object loop in PDF page label tree
item.description: In Xpdf 4.04 (and earlier), a PDF object loop in the page label tree leads to infinite recursion and a stack overflow.

item.title: CVE-2023-2662 · 5.5 MEDIUM — Divide-by-zero in Xpdf 4.04 due to bad color space object
item.description: In Xpdf 4.04 (and earlier), a bad color space object in the input PDF file can cause a divide-by-zero.
